name: Southwest Jewelry Buyers
description_short: Scottsdale-based jewelry and gold buyer since 2000. BBB A+ accredited, 13-time Best of Scottsdale winner, specializing in diamonds, gold, platinum, and luxury watches.
description_long:
Southwest Jewelry Buyers has operated from Scottsdale's Airpark district since its founding in 2000, making it one of the longest-running independent precious metals and jewelry buyers in the Phoenix metro area. The company is fully licensed by the City of Scottsdale and holds both an accreditation and an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau. It has been featured on Sonoran Living on ABC15 Arizona, giving it a level of regional media credibility that few single-location buyers in the state can claim.

The company purchases a wide range of precious items: gold, silver, platinum, and diamond jewelry; loose diamonds from 1 carat to 10 or more carats; Rolex and luxury watches; coins; and general fine jewelry. Every transaction begins with a free, zero-obligation appraisal — sellers are under no pressure to accept any offer. All appointments are conducted Monday through Friday at the Scottsdale Airpark office, in a private, upscale environment the company describes as fully confidential.

Southwest Jewelry Buyers has accumulated over 1,000 five-star Google reviews and more than 100 Yelp reviews — an unusually high volume for a single-location buyer. They have won the Best of Scottsdale award for 13 consecutive years, a distinction reflecting sustained customer satisfaction over more than a decade. The firm's appointment-only model and emphasis on privacy set it apart from traditional storefront pawn shops; the experience is closer to a private dealer or estate buyer than a retail exchange.

It is important to understand that Southwest Jewelry Buyers purchases items outright — this is not a pawn or collateral-loan service. Sellers permanently transfer ownership in exchange for a cash payment; there is no option to reclaim items later. The weekday-only, appointment-only schedule can be a real obstacle for people who cannot take time off work. No pricing benchmarks or indicative rates are published on the website, so sellers have no way to gauge expected payouts before visiting. Those who are comfortable with in-person negotiation and are seeking a well-credentialed local buyer for quality pieces will find this among the most reputable options in the Scottsdale area.
pros: Over 25 years in operation — founded in 2000, one of the longest-established jewelry buyers in Scottsdale
BBB A+ accreditation with independent verification of ethical business practices
Won Best of Scottsdale award 13 consecutive years — sustained, measurable local recognition
Over 1,000 five-star Google reviews and 100+ Yelp reviews — unusually high volume for a single-location buyer
Licensed by the City of Scottsdale — regulatory compliance independently confirmed
Free, zero-obligation quotes — no pressure to sell after appraisal
Featured on ABC15 Arizona (Sonoran Living) — regional TV credibility
cons: Weekday-only, appointment-only hours (Monday–Friday) — inaccessible for most 9-to-5 workers
Outright purchase only — no pawn or collateral-loan option; sellers cannot reclaim items after sale
Single Scottsdale Airpark location — no additional Phoenix metro branches for broader access
No pricing transparency online — sellers cannot benchmark expected payouts before visiting
No remote or online appraisal option mentioned — in-person visit required for all quotes
best_for: Sellers with high-value diamond jewelry (1 carat or larger) seeking a specialized buyer
Rolex and luxury watch owners who want a private, upscale selling environment
People liquidating inherited gold or platinum jewelry who prioritize discretion
Scottsdale and Phoenix Valley residents who can schedule weekday appointments and want a long-established local buyer over a national chain
diagnosis: Southwest Jewelry Buyers is best suited for Phoenix Valley residents looking to sell high-value jewelry, diamonds, or luxury watches to a long-established, highly-rated local buyer in a private setting. The primary caveat is that this is an outright-purchase business with no loan or reclaim option, and the weekday-only appointment requirement makes it impractical for anyone who cannot visit during business hours.
